technique: Cortical suspensory fixation via adjustable-loop TightRope II RT. FlipCutter III for retrograde femoral tunnel drilling. GraftLink all-inside technique available. BioACL biologic augmentation option. Autograft (BTB, hamstring) or allograft based on patient demand.

product: ACL Repair TightRope® Implant with FiberRing™ Sutures
technique: Primary ACL repair using FiberRing sutures through native ligament with TightRope cortical suspensory fixation and InternalBrace FiberTape augmentation. Best for proximal femoral avulsions in skeletally mature patients within 3 weeks of injury.
contraindications: poor tissue quality on intraoperative assessment

technique: Tibial inlay or transtibial tunnel technique. TightRope II RT cortical fixation on femoral side. Achilles allograft or doubled semitendinosus autograft preferred. Staged if combined with other ligament injuries.

FiberTape InternalBrace spanning two SwiveLock knotless anchors augments primary MCL suture repair. Expands area of tissue approximation to reduce failure rates. Shorter immobilization and faster return to play versus suture-only repair.
contraindications: Stener-type lesion requiring formal open repair

technique: All-inside repair using FasT-Fix 360 polyetheretherketone implants with pre-tied sliding knots. Zone-specific cannulas for posterior horn access. Inside-out suture technique for peripheral red-red and red-white zone tears.
contraindications: degenerative horizontal cleavage tear in older patient

technique: Transtibial pull-through technique using FlipCutter II to create tibial socket at root footprint. SutureLoc knotless implant for direct tibial fixation. 2.4 mm cannulated drill preserves bone stock. Retensionable knotless construct. Multiple suture pass patterns.
contraindications: severe meniscal extrusion with established OA

technique: Dovetail or keyhole technique for lateral/medial meniscal allograft transplantation. Semi-trapezoidal recipient slot created in tibia; peripheral fixation with 2-0 FiberWire® to capsular rim. Graft size matched via pre-op imaging.

Anatomic MPFL reconstruction using gracilis autograft + FiberTape InternalBrace augmentation. Dual superomedial patella anchor fixation with SwiveLock. Femoral attachment at Schottle point under fluoroscopic guidance. Concurrent TTO if TT-TG >20 mm or alta present.
contraindications: fixed bony malalignment requiring TTO (address concurrently)

product: T3 AMZ™ System for Tibial Tuberosity Osteotomy
technique: Fixed-angle (45°, 60°, 90°) cutting guides provide precise control of anteriorization and medialization. Corrects patellofemoral malalignment and unloads lateral patellar facet. Soft-tissue retractor protects posterior structures.

Opening wedge proximal tibial osteotomy with anatomically contoured ContourLock HTO plate. iBalance PEEK implants as screw-free alternative for correction fixation. BoneSync or OSferion wedges for void filling.

Lateral distal femoral opening wedge osteotomy via diaphyseal-metaphyseal flare. Femoral Osteotomy Plate fixation with proximal bicortical and distal cancellous screws. Orthobiologic bone void filler as needed.

product: Knee Capsule Implant System (Knotless SutureTak® + GAP™ Guide)
technique: Two knotless SutureTak percutaneous anchors placed 3 mm below medial tibial joint line via GAP guided arthroscopic placement guide. Restores meniscotibial ligament integrity to reduce medial meniscal extrusion and improve load distribution.

Micronized allograft cartilage scaffold placed after standard microfracture preparation. Mixed with autologous PRP and sealed with fibrin glue. Addresses focal defects 1–5 cm². Concurrent HTO or TTO if malalignment present.

Single or multiple osteochondral cylindrical plug harvest from low-load articular surface (lateral trochlea) with press-fit transfer to recipient defect. Mosaicplasty for defects 2–4 cm². Concurrent microfracture for donor sites.

product: Arthrex BioComposite Chondral Dart™ / CDF Pins
technique: Arthroscopic reduction and fixation of osteochondral fragments using BioComposite Chondral Darts or headless variable-pitch compression screws. Retrograde drilling for unstable lesions. Bone grafting of defect base.
contraindications: fragment >2.5 cm without viable subchondral bone base

technique: FiberTak SpeedBridge technique: 2.6 mm FiberTak RC soft anchors for three-point medial-row fixation, lateral row knotless fixation with SwiveLock anchors and FiberTape. Knotless construct eliminates knot impingement risk. Subacromial decompression performed adjunctively.
contraindications: massive irreparable tear (consider CuffMend augmentation or reverse TSA)

technique: ArthroFlex dermal allograft introduced with graft spreader. FiberStitch RC implant for medial soft-tissue fixation to rotator cuff tendon. Self-punching PushLock anchors for lateral bony footprint fixation. Designed for completion in under 10 minutes.

Arthroscopic Bankart repair via anteroinferior portal with SwiveLock knotless anchors. Hill-Sachs remplissage with knotless tensionable anchors for engaging lesions. Suture tape loop stitch configuration for labral capture. Capsular plication as needed.

Two knotless tensionable SwiveLock anchors placed in Hill-Sachs defect to inset posterior capsule and infraspinatus tendon. Performed in combination with arthroscopic Bankart repair. Knotless technique minimizes knot impingement and migration.

AC TightRope device (round clavicle button + oblong coracoid button) for coracoclavicular ligament reconstruction. Twin Tail TightRope for Grade IV–V injuries. Dog Bone button alternative for chronic AC instability. Allograft augmentation for chronic cases.

Arthroscopic repair of superior labral tear with SwiveLock knotless anchors. Posterior portal viewing with anterosuperior working portal. Suture passer through labral tissue prior to anchor insertion. Consider biceps tenodesis for Type II–IV failure or patient >40 years.

product: SwiveLock® Biceps Tenodesis System / TensionTight™ Button
technique: Arthroscopic suprapectoral or open subpectoral biceps tenodesis using SwiveLock anchor or BioTenodesis interference screw. TensionTight knotless onlay technique as alternative. Loop 'N' Tack stitch for all-arthroscopic approach. Tenotomy for low-demand patients.
contraindications: severe concurrent rotator cuff requiring full attention

technique: Stemmed or stemless anatomic TSA with glenoid resurfacing. Patient-specific instrumentation available. Convertible to reverse configuration if cuff deficient. Deltopectoral approach with subscapularis management.
contraindications: severe rotator cuff deficiency (use reverse TSA)

technique: Reverse prosthesis with medialized center of rotation and lateralized humerus. Deltoid-powered elevation compensates for cuff deficiency. Lateralized offset glenosphere option. FiberTape cerclage available for tuberosity management.
contraindications: insufficient glenoid or humeral bone stock

technique: Arthroscopic bursectomy, acromioplasty, and coracoacromial ligament release. Posterior viewing portal with lateral working portal. Performed concurrently with rotator cuff repair when indicated.
contraindications: full-thickness rotator cuff tear requiring repair (adjunct only)

product: SutureTak® / FiberTak® / PushLock® Hip Labral Anchors
technique: Arthroscopic labral repair using SutureTak, FiberTak, or PushLock suture anchors into acetabulum. Circumferential or labral base stitch technique. Concurrent cam/pincer osteoplasty via burr. Hip Labral Scorpion and SwiftStitch suture passers for efficient tissue capture. Capsular closure with CapsuleClose Scorpion.

Circumferential or segmental labral reconstruction using iliotibial band or gracilis allograft when native labrum is beyond repair. Anchor fixation to acetabular rim with FiberTak/SutureTak. Concurrent cam/pincer correction.

Open or endoscopic approach with identification and protection of sciatic nerve. Ischial tuberosity debrided and re-approximated. Tendinous hamstring origins reattached using 3–4 SwiveLock knotless anchors. Knotless construct reduces implant prominence.
contraindications: chronic avulsion >3 months (reconstruction with allograft may be needed)

product: Arthrex Hip Anchor System with FiberTape®
technique: Endoscopic or open gluteus medius repair to greater trochanter using SwiveLock knotless anchors with FiberTape InternalBrace augmentation. Trochanteric bursectomy performed. PRP injection for isolated trochanteric pain syndrome.
contraindications: massive irreparable gluteus medius tear (allograft reconstruction required)

product: Arthrex Elbow UCL Reconstruction Instrument Set
technique: Docking technique UCL reconstruction with palmaris longus or gracilis graft via Arthrex UCL Reconstruction Instrument Set. Supports Modified Jobe, Docking, and DANE TJ techniques. FiberWire and FiberLoop for graft stitching. Interference screw for ulna fixation. Ulnar nerve transposition if symptomatic.
contraindications: mild sprains amenable to InternalBrace repair

technique: Primary UCL repair augmented with InternalBrace FiberTape between two 3.5 mm PEEK SwiveLock anchors on humerus and ulna. Eliminates secondary hardware removal. Best for young athletes with isolated proximal or distal UCL avulsion, acute injury, no chronic attritional damage.

Tension-slide technique via single anterior incision. BicepsButton cortical fixation with intramedullary passage through radius to radial tuberosity. Tenodesis screw added for aperture fixation and anatomic tensioning. FiberTak Biceps Implant System as all-suture anchor alternative.

product: Arthrex TENEX™ Percutaneous Tenotomy / Open Nirschl Kit
technique: Ultrasound-guided percutaneous TENEX needle tenotomy of angiofibroblastic ECRB tissue or open Nirschl technique with debridement of degenerate origin. Minimally invasive approach allows earlier return to activity.
contraindications: less than 6 months of failed conservative treatment (PT, injections, NSAIDs)

product: PARS Achilles Midsubstance SpeedBridge™ Implant System
technique: Minimally invasive PARS jig technique for percutaneous suture passage, combined with SpeedBridge 4-anchor knotless construct. FiberTape hourglass pattern over distal tendon onto calcaneus provides greater compression area than single-anchor fixation. Immediate postoperative weight bearing and range of motion possible.
contraindications: chronic neglected rupture >6 weeks (open reconstruction or FHL transfer)

technique: Brostrom-Gould primary ATFL repair augmented with FiberTape InternalBrace from fibula to talus and calcaneus. Talus offset guide ensures reproducible anatomic SwiveLock anchor placement. Expanded area of tissue approximation enables early weight bearing. Applicable to acute and chronic ankle sprains and revisions.
contraindications: tissue quality inadequate for Brostrom (consider allograft reconstruction)
peroneal tendon pathology requiring concurrent repair

technique: TightRope suture-button device for tibiofibular syndesmosis stabilization. Dynamic fixation preserves physiologic motion and eliminates need for hardware removal. Biomechanically superior to syndesmotic screw with lower malreduction risk.
contraindications: associated fibular fracture requiring plate fixation (combine with plating)

technique: Peroneal tendon tubularization and longitudinal tear repair via retromalleolar approach. Superior peroneal retinaculum repair with SwiveLock anchors to posterior fibula. Fibular groove deepening if retromalleolar sulcus insufficient.
contraindications: severe tendon degeneration requiring allograft reconstruction

product: Arthrex FHL Implant System / BioComposite Tenodesis Screw
technique: Flexor hallucis longus tendon harvested and traced to calcaneus. Transferred to posterior calcaneus via tenodesis tension-slide technique. DX button for plantar cortical fixation; BioComposite tenodesis screw for aperture fixation. Can be combined with spring ligament InternalBrace augmentation.
contraindications: hallux weakness unacceptable to patient (discuss trade-off)

technique: Arthroscopic debridement of anterior tibiotalar osteophytes using shaver and burr. Anteromedial and anterolateral portals. Loose body removal and synovectomy. Talar osteochondral lesion treated concurrently if present.
contraindications: severe ankle osteoarthritis (consider fusion or arthroplasty)

technique: Percutaneous minimally invasive bunionectomy via trajectory system with beveled fully threaded zero-profile screws. Correction and fixation via percutaneous approach. Dedicated power unit and instrumentation provided.
contraindications: severe hallux valgus with articular incongruence (open procedure preferred)

technique: Primary ligamentous Lisfranc injury: suture-button fixation (TightRope technique) across first and second tarsometatarsal articulation. Bony Lisfranc fracture-dislocation: ORIF with screws or bridging plates. Primary fusion considered for purely ligamentous injuries.

product: Forefoot InternalBrace™ Ligament Augmentation Repair Implant System
technique: All-in-one sterile system with 3 mm × 8 mm tenodesis screw implants, oblong button, FiberTape and LabralTape sutures. Knotless no-profile repair construct. Augments plantar plate or crossover digit collateral ligament repairs.

product: Centerline™ Endoscopic Carpal Tunnel Release System
technique: Centerline single-portal endoscopic release of transverse carpal ligament through limited wrist flexor crease incision. Direct visualization of deep side of ligament before blade elevation. Thumb trigger pull for controlled single-handed release. NanoScopic all-in-one sterile system alternative. Up to 50% earlier return to activity versus open technique.

product: Titanium Volar Distal Radius Plating System
technique: Volar approach locked plate fixation with Titanium Wrist Plating System. Anatomically contoured plates in narrow/standard/wide and multiple shaft lengths. KreuLock variable-angle locking screws for comminuted and intra-articular patterns. Fluoroscopic guidance throughout.
contraindications: non-displaced stable fractures acceptable for splinting

product: DX SwiveLock® SL Anchor + InternalBrace™ Augmentation
technique: Dorsal reconstruction with 3.5 mm DX SwiveLock SL anchor for dynamic and static scapholunate instability. Biologic tendon graft reconstruction into bony tunnels with InternalBrace static suture reinforcement. Indicated when ligament is beyond repair, carpal cartilage preserved, and carpus is reducible.

product: Mini TightRope® CMC / LRTI with BioComposite Tenodesis Screw
technique: Trapeziectomy followed by FCR tendon LRTI (ligament reconstruction tendon interposition) with BioComposite tenodesis screw for aperture fixation. Mini TightRope metacarpal suspension as adjunct or alternative to maintain first–second metacarpal relationship and prevent proximal migration.

Modified Kessler or Adelaide 4-strand core suture using FiberLoop with running epitendinous repair using FiberWire. Flexor tendon: early Duran controlled passive motion protocol. Extensor tendon: yoke or figure-of-eight repair based on zone. Early mobilization critical for zone 2 outcomes.
contraindications: severe tissue loss (staged reconstruction with silicone rod)

technique: Percutaneous or open reduction with Acutrak 2 headless variable-pitch compression screw placed along central axis under fluoroscopic guidance. Variable pitch provides self-compression across fracture site. Vascularized bone graft (1,2-ICSRA or free medial femoral condyle) for non-union or proximal pole avascular necrosis.
contraindications: avascular necrosis of proximal pole (vascularized graft required)

technique: Ultrasound-guided injection of autologous platelet-rich plasma prepared via ACP double-syringe system. Leukocyte-poor preparation for intra-articular joint injections; leukocyte-rich for tendinopathy. 1–3 injection series. First-line or adjunct to surgical intervention.

Fluoroscopic or ultrasound-guided ACP/PRP injection into cervical facet joints (typically C3-C7) for facet arthropathy and axial cervical pain. Leukocyte-poor platelet-rich plasma reduces inflammatory load at the facet capsule. 1–3 injection series spaced 4–6 weeks apart. Effective first-line interventional option for cervical facet syndrome, spondylosis, and rotational pain restrictions before surgical consideration.

product: Arthrex ACP® Biologics + Spine Specialist Referral
technique: Conservative pathway: structured physical therapy, epidural steroid injection, Arthrex ACP/PRP biologics for soft tissue healing. Surgical pathway (ACDF or disc arthroplasty) indicated after 6–12 weeks of failed conservative management with persistent neurological deficit or imaging-confirmed cord/root compression. Arthrex BioACL biologic augmentation used adjunctively for anterior cervical procedures to support fusion and soft tissue healing.
contraindications: isolated axial neck pain without neurological signs (conservative first)
adjacent segment disease requiring full evaluation

procedure: Cervical Soft Tissue / Ligament InternalBrace Augmentation
technique: Augmentation of cervical soft tissue and ligamentous structures using Arthrex FiberTape InternalBrace construct for chronic cervical instability, particularly following whiplash or hypermobility disorders. Knotless SwiveLock anchor-based fixation to restore dynamic cervical stability. Combined with structured rehabilitation and ACP/PRP biologic augmentation postoperatively.
contraindications: acute cervical fracture requiring stabilization first

technique: Cervical spinal stenosis with myelopathy requires surgical decompression (posterior laminoplasty, laminectomy, or anterior corpectomy). Arthrex ACP/PRP biologics used perioperatively to support recovery. Arthrex soft tissue implants applied for dural and ligamentous repair as needed. Urgency determined by severity of myelopathic signs on examination and MRI findings.
contraindications: isolated cervical radiculopathy without myelopathy (less invasive options preferred)

technique: Conservative pathway: structured physical therapy, NSAIDs, epidural steroid injections, Arthrex ACP/PRP biologics for disc healing support. Surgical pathway (microdiscectomy via posterior laminotomy or endoscopic discectomy) indicated for persistent radiculopathy >6 weeks, progressive neurological deficit, or cauda equina symptoms. Arthrex biologics used adjunctively perioperatively.

product: Arthrex ACP® Biologics / Surgical Specialist Referral
technique: Surgical decompression via single-level or multilevel laminectomy or laminotomy, with or without fusion depending on instability. Minimally invasive tubular decompression technique reduces muscle disruption. Arthrex ACP/PRP biologics used perioperatively for enhanced tissue healing. Physical therapy and epidural steroid injections as conservative bridging.

product: Arthrex Pedicle Screw / Posterior Fusion System
technique: Conservative management: core stabilization exercises, bracing, ACP/PRP biologics for pain modulation. Surgical: posterior spinal fusion (TLIF or PLIF) with pedicle screw instrumentation and interbody cage for grades II+ or progressive slip. Decompression (laminectomy/foraminotomy) concurrent with fusion. ACP biologics enhance fusion biology.
contraindications: multi-level disease requiring extended construct planning

procedure: Sacroiliac Joint Injection / SI Joint Fusion
product: ACP® Double-Syringe PRP System / SI Joint Fusion Implants
technique: Fluoroscopic or CT-guided ACP/PRP or corticosteroid injection into sacroiliac joint as first-line intervention. Prolotherapy and ACP biological series for chronic SI ligamentous laxity. Minimally invasive SI joint fusion (lateral transarticular technique) indicated for refractory mechanical SI pain after >3 months of failed conservative care.
contraindications: fracture of sacrum or ilium (address first)

technique: Fluoroscopic or ultrasound-guided ACP/PRP injection into lumbar facet joints (L3–S1) for facet arthropathy and axial lumbar pain. Leukocyte-poor PRP preparation reduces inflammatory cytokine load. Medial branch nerve block series for diagnostic confirmation prior to radiofrequency ablation. 1–3 injection series spaced 4–6 weeks apart.
contraindications: radicular symptoms suggesting disc herniation (address separately)

technique: Balloon kyphoplasty: bilateral transpedicular approach with inflation of bone tamps to restore vertebral height before polymethylmethacrylate (PMMA) cement injection. Vertebroplasty: direct PMMA injection for stabilization without height restoration. Arthrex ACP/PRP biologics used adjunctively. Indicated for acute osteoporotic compression fractures with severe pain refractory to conservative management.
contraindications: posterior cortex disruption with canal compromise (open stabilization)

procedure: Posterior Spinal Fusion (PSF) with Pedicle Screws
product: Arthrex Pedicle Screw & Rod System / ACP® Biologics
technique: Posterior pedicle screw and rod construct with interbody fusion cage (TLIF or PLIF technique) for lumbar instability, spondylolisthesis, or degenerative disc disease failing conservative management. Percutaneous minimally invasive (MIS) approach reduces muscle disruption and blood loss. Arthrex ACP/PRP biologics augment fusion biology and accelerate bone graft incorporation.
contraindications: significant medical comorbidities precluding prolonged anesthesia
single-level disease responsive to conservative care

technique: In-situ ulnar nerve decompression via open medial approach through cubital tunnel retinaculum, Osborne's ligament, and arcade of Struthers. Endoscopic technique available for isolated compression. Anterior subcutaneous or submuscular transposition added for recurrent or severe cases. ACP/PRP biologics applied to nerve bed.
contraindications: mild cubital tunnel responsive to conservative measures (splinting, activity modification)
significant medial elbow instability requiring concurrent UCL repair

product: Arthrex Elbow Tension Band & Plating System
technique: Displaced olecranon fracture: posterior approach with tension band wiring or pre-contoured locking plate fixation. Low-profile plate preferred to minimize symptomatic hardware. Bursectomy performed for isolated olecranon bursitis via excision or aspiration. ACP/PRP biologics for acute management of non-displaced fractures or refractory bursitis.
contraindications: active infection of bursa (aspiration + antibiotics first)

product: Arthrex Radial Head Implant / Mini Fragment Plating System
technique: Mason Type I-II: non-operative management or mini-fragment ORIF via Kocher lateral approach. Mason Type III (comminuted): radial head arthroplasty with press-fit prosthesis. Arthrex mini fragment plating system for two-part fractures amenable to fixation. Concurrent assessment for associated ligamentous injury (Essex-Lopresti, PLRI).
contraindications: non-displaced Mason Type I (splinting + early motion)

product: Arthrex Elbow LUCL Reconstruction System (SwiveLock® + FiberTape®)
technique: Open lateral approach to reconstruct LUCL using palmaris longus graft through humeral and ulnar osseous tunnels with InternalBrace FiberTape augmentation and SwiveLock knotless anchors. Plication or direct repair for acute avulsions. Arthrex LUCL Reconstruction Instrument Set provides reproducible isometric graft positioning.
contraindications: global elbow instability requiring staged reconstruction

technique: Total hip arthroplasty via posterior, direct anterior, or direct lateral approach. Cementless press-fit acetabular cup with polyethylene or ceramic liner and femoral stem. Robotic-assisted or computer navigation for optimized component positioning. Direct anterior minimally invasive approach reduces abductor disruption and accelerates rehabilitation. ACP/PRP biologics perioperatively for enhanced recovery.
contraindications: insufficient bone stock for cementless fixation (cemented technique)

product: Arthrex Hip Arthroscopy System / ACP® Biologics
technique: Arthroscopic or endoscopic iliopsoas tendon release at the lesser trochanter or zona orbicularis level. ACP/PRP injection into iliopsoas bursa for non-operative management of tendinopathy. Hip arthroscopy allows concurrent treatment of labral pathology. Eccentric strengthening rehabilitation postoperatively.
contraindications: less than 3 months conservative management (PT, injections)

technique: Arthroscopic microfracture for focal full-thickness cartilage defects <2 cm². AMIC (Autologous Matrix-Induced Chondrogenesis) with collagen scaffold over microfracture base. Osteochondral allograft or autograft (OATS) for larger defects. ACP/PRP biologics augment chondral healing. Early AVN of femoral head addressed with core decompression and biologic augmentation.

product: Arthrex Pelvic Plating & Screw Fixation System
technique: Displaced acetabular fractures: ORIF via ilioinguinal, Kocher-Langenbeck, or modified Stoppa approach based on fracture pattern. Percutaneous screw fixation for minimally displaced patterns. Pelvic ring disruptions: anterior plating + posterior iliosacral screw fixation. Arthrex anatomically contoured plates and variable-angle locking screws.
contraindications: severe soft tissue compromise precluding open approach (staged)

product: Arthrex / Partner — Cervical Disc Arthroplasty System
technique: Motion-preserving anterior cervical disc arthroplasty for 1–2 level symptomatic cervical disc disease. Anterior cervical approach with disc excision and end plate preparation. Prosthesis maintains segmental motion and reduces adjacent segment stress versus ACDF fusion. ACP/PRP biologics for soft tissue healing postoperatively. Indicated for patients preferring motion preservation over fusion.
contraindications: osteoporosis with insufficient end plate quality
advanced facet arthropathy at index level
cervical myelopathy with canal compromise (may require decompression + fusion)

keywords: neck pain arm pain one side
product: Arthrex ACP® Biologics / Surgical Specialist Referral
technique: Posterior cervical keyhole laminoforaminotomy via minimally invasive tubular or open approach for unilateral foraminal stenosis. Avoids anterior approach and fusion. Partial facetectomy to decompress exiting nerve root. ACP/PRP biologics to support soft tissue healing. Suitable for soft disc herniation or foraminal osteophyte causing unilateral radiculopathy.
contraindications: bilateral symptoms requiring multilevel decompression (anterior approach preferred)
cervical myelopathy with cord signal change

technique: Fluoroscopic interlaminar or transforaminal cervical epidural steroid or ACP/PRP injection for cervical radiculopathy and axial neck pain. Contrast injection (epidurogram) confirms spread before therapeutic injection. ACP biologics as an alternative or adjunct to steroid for disc and nerve healing. 1–3 injection series. Avoids systemic steroid side effects.

product: Arthrex TFCC Repair System (FiberWire® / SwiveLock® Anchors)
technique: Arthroscopic TFCC repair: peripheral tears repaired via outside-in or inside-out suture technique using FiberWire through 3-4 and 6-R portals. Central degenerative tears: debridement. DRUJ instability: open reconstruction of dorsal and volar radioulnar ligaments with SwiveLock anchors. Ulnar shortening osteotomy for ulnar impaction syndrome.
contraindications: irreparable central tear in older patient (debridement preferred)
ulna minus variant without impaction (less likely to benefit from UO)

technique: Diagnostic and operative wrist arthroscopy via standard 3-4, 4-5, 6-R, and MCR portals. Synovectomy and debridement of chondral lesions. Loose body removal. ACP/PRP biologics applied intra-articular for early degenerative changes. Concurrent TFCC or ligament repair as needed. Traction tower or suspension technique.
contraindications: significant soft tissue pathology better addressed by open approach

product: Arthrex First Dorsal Compartment Release Kit
technique: Open or ultrasound-guided percutaneous first dorsal compartment release at the radial styloid. Careful subcompartment identification to release all APL and EPB slips (2–4 tendons). ACP/PRP injection as first-line treatment for mild-moderate tenosynovitis. Postoperative thumb spica splinting for 2 weeks followed by progressive range of motion.
contraindications: less than 6 weeks of failed conservative care (splinting, NSAIDs, steroid injection)
concurrent scaphoid pathology or CMC arthritis requiring separate management
